healthBenefits: High protein, low fat, lean meat

metadata.description: NutriWise comprehensive food dataset — Indian + international foods with full nutrition, recipes, and meal plan templates

foods.name: Pav Bhaji Masala (1 bowl, no bread)
Egg Boiled with Chaat Masala (2 eggs)

recipes.name: Classic Masala Dosa
recipes.description: Crispy South Indian crepe filled with spiced potato filling, served with coconut chutney and sambar

ingredients.notes: use ready batter or ferment overnight

steps.title: Prepare filling
steps.instruction: Heat 1 tbsp oil in a pan. Add mustard seeds and let them splutter. Add curry leaves, green chillies, and onion. Sauté until onion turns golden.

steps.title: Season potatoes
steps.instruction: Add turmeric, mashed potatoes, and salt. Mix well and cook for 2 minutes. Add coriander leaves and keep aside.

steps.title: Make dosa
steps.instruction: Heat a cast iron or non-stick pan on high. Pour a ladle of batter and spread in a circular motion to a thin crepe. Drizzle ½ tsp oil on edges.

steps.title: Fill and fold
steps.instruction: When dosa turns golden and crispy, place 2-3 tbsp potato filling in the center. Fold into a half-moon or roll. Serve hot with chutney and sambar.

tips: Batter consistency should be like thin buttermilk
Pan must be very hot for crispy dosa
Spread batter quickly in one circular motion
recipes.nutrition_notes: Rich in carbs and fiber. Fermentation increases B12 and probiotic content.

recipes.name: High-Protein Moong Dal Chilla
recipes.description: Savory protein-rich green moong pancakes — a perfect diabetic-friendly breakfast

steps.title: Grind batter
steps.instruction: Blend soaked moong dal with ginger, green chilli, and ¼ cup water to a smooth, thick batter (similar to idli batter consistency).

steps.title: Add vegetables
steps.instruction: Transfer to a bowl. Add chopped onion, tomato, coriander, cumin seeds, and salt. Mix well.

steps.title: Cook chilla
steps.instruction: Heat a non-stick pan on medium-high. Pour a ladle of batter and spread into a circle. Add ½ tsp oil on edges. Cook until edges lift, then flip. Cook other side 1 min.

steps.title: Serve
steps.instruction: Serve hot with mint-coriander chutney or hung curd dip.

tips: Batter should be thick to get fluffy chillas
Add spinach or grated carrot for extra nutrition
Works great as a post-workout meal
recipes.nutrition_notes: Excellent source of plant protein and fiber. Low GI makes it ideal for diabetics.

recipes.name: Rajma Chawal (Kidney Bean Curry with Rice)
recipes.description: Hearty North Indian kidney bean curry served over steamed rice — a complete plant-based meal

steps.title: Pressure cook rajma
steps.instruction: Drain soaked rajma. Pressure cook with 3 cups water and ½ tsp salt for 4-5 whistles until completely soft.

steps.title: Make onion base
steps.instruction: Heat oil in a pan. Add cumin seeds and bay leaf. Add onions and sauté until golden brown (8-10 minutes). Add ginger-garlic paste and cook 2 more minutes.

steps.title: Add spices
steps.instruction: Add tomato puree and all dry spices. Cook on medium heat, stirring often, until oil separates from masala (8-10 minutes).

steps.title: Combine
steps.instruction: Add cooked rajma with its cooking water. Mix well. Simmer for 10-15 minutes until gravy thickens. Add garam masala and adjust salt.

steps.title: Serve
steps.instruction: Serve hot over cooked rice. Top with a dollop of butter (optional) and chopped coriander.

tips: Use canned rajma to cut prep time
Slow-cooking on low flame develops deeper flavor
Freezes well — make a large batch for meal prep
recipes.nutrition_notes: Complete protein when paired with rice. Very high in fiber and iron.

recipes.name: Grilled Chicken with Ragi Roti
recipes.description: Lean grilled chicken breast with calcium-rich millet roti — ideal for muscle building

steps.title: Marinate chicken
steps.instruction: Mix lemon juice, ginger-garlic paste, cumin, chilli flakes, oil, salt, and pepper. Coat chicken breast well. Marinate 30 mins (or overnight in fridge).

steps.title: Make ragi roti
steps.instruction: Mix ragi flour with salt and warm water to form a smooth dough. Divide into 4 balls. Flatten each ball on a damp cloth or plastic sheet and transfer to a hot griddle.

steps.title: Cook roti
steps.instruction: Cook ragi roti on medium heat, pressing gently with a cloth. Flip when edges start drying. Cook 1-2 mins each side. Brush with ¼ tsp ghee (optional).

steps.title: Grill chicken
steps.instruction: Heat a grill pan on high. Grill marinated chicken 5-6 minutes per side until cooked through and char marks form. Rest for 3 minutes before slicing.

steps.title: Serve
steps.instruction: Slice chicken and serve alongside ragi rotis with mint chutney and sliced onion.

tips: Pound chicken to even thickness for uniform cooking
Ragi dough is sticky — wet hands help
Add curd to marinade for more tender chicken
recipes.nutrition_notes: High complete protein from chicken. Ragi provides calcium and iron. Great post-workout meal.

recipes.name: Oats & Vegetable Porridge
recipes.description: Savory oats loaded with vegetables — a diabetes-friendly, heart-healthy breakfast bowl

steps.title: Temper spices
steps.instruction: Heat oil in a pan. Add mustard seeds, let them splutter. Add curry leaves, green chilli, and onion. Sauté 2 minutes.

steps.title: Add vegetables
steps.instruction: Add grated carrot, tomato, and spinach. Cook 2 minutes until slightly soft.

steps.title: Cook oats
steps.instruction: Add oats and water (or milk). Stir well. Add salt and cumin powder. Cook on medium heat, stirring frequently, until oats absorb liquid (4-5 mins).

steps.title: Serve
steps.instruction: Serve hot. Add a squeeze of lemon and fresh coriander on top.

tips: Use steel-cut oats for lower GI
Add a boiled egg on the side for extra protein
Works great with coconut milk for a South Indian flavor
recipes.nutrition_notes: Beta-glucan in oats actively lowers LDL cholesterol. High fiber keeps blood sugar stable.

recipes.name: Palak Dal (Spinach Lentil Curry)
recipes.description: Iron-rich spinach and masoor dal curry — excellent for anemia and everyday nutrition

ingredients.notes: squeeze at end for iron absorption

steps.title: Cook dal
steps.instruction: Wash masoor dal. Pressure cook with 2 cups water, turmeric, and salt for 2 whistles. It should be completely soft and mushy.

steps.title: Make tempering
steps.instruction: Heat oil in a pan. Add cumin seeds. Add onions and sauté golden. Add ginger-garlic paste, cook 2 mins. Add tomatoes and cook until soft.

steps.title: Add spices
steps.instruction: Add coriander powder, chilli powder, and amchur. Cook masala 2-3 mins until oil separates.

steps.title: Add spinach
steps.instruction: Add washed spinach leaves. Cook 2 minutes until wilted.

steps.title: Combine
steps.instruction: Add cooked dal and mix well. Simmer 5 minutes. Add water to adjust consistency. Finish with lemon juice.

tips: Add lemon after removing from heat to preserve Vitamin C (helps iron absorption)
Use baby spinach for smoother texture
Fenugreek leaves (methi) can replace half the spinach for blood sugar benefits
recipes.nutrition_notes: Masoor dal is one of the highest iron legumes. Spinach adds additional iron and folate. Vitamin C from lemon dramatically increases iron absorption.

recipes.name: Vegetable Sambar
recipes.description: Classic South Indian lentil and vegetable stew — a nutritional powerhouse

steps.title: Cook dal
steps.instruction: Pressure cook toor dal with turmeric and 1½ cups water for 3 whistles. Mash well.

steps.title: Soak tamarind
steps.instruction: Soak tamarind in 1 cup warm water for 10 mins. Squeeze and extract juice.

steps.title: Cook vegetables
steps.instruction: In a pot, add tamarind water, vegetables (drumstick, brinjal, onions, tomato), sambar powder, and salt. Boil until vegetables are cooked (10-12 mins).

steps.title: Add dal
steps.instruction: Add mashed dal to the vegetable pot. Mix well. Adjust consistency and salt. Simmer 5 minutes.

steps.title: Temper
steps.instruction: Heat oil in a small pan. Add mustard seeds, dried chillies, curry leaves, and hing. Pour this tempering over sambar.

tips: Drumstick is essential for authentic sambar flavor
Use ghee for tempering for a richer taste
Keeps for 2 days in fridge — flavors improve overnight
recipes.nutrition_notes: Toor dal is high in protein and fiber. Drumstick leaves are one of the most nutrient-dense vegetables available.

recipes.name: Sprouts Salad Bowl
recipes.description: Protein-packed raw sprouts salad with fresh vegetables and tangy dressing

steps.title: Prepare sprouts
steps.instruction: Soak moong/moth beans overnight, drain, and keep in a damp cloth for 1-2 days to sprout. Or use ready sprouts from market.

steps.title: Combine
steps.instruction: In a bowl, combine sprouts, tomato, cucumber, onion, and green chilli.

steps.title: Dress
steps.instruction: Add lemon juice, chaat masala, cumin powder, and salt. Toss well. Garnish with coriander.

tips: Lightly steam sprouts if you have digestive sensitivity
Add pomegranate seeds for extra antioxidants
Best eaten fresh — do not store once dressed
recipes.nutrition_notes: Sprouting increases protein, vitamins, and makes nutrients easier to absorb. Excellent for blood sugar management.

recipes.name: Chettinad Chicken Curry
recipes.description: Boldly spiced Tamil Nadu chicken curry with freshly ground masala

steps.title: Grind masala
steps.instruction: Dry roast kalpasi, marathi mokku, fennel, and black pepper for 2 mins. Grind with coconut to a fine paste with a little water.

steps.title: Cook onions
steps.instruction: Heat oil. Fry curry leaves. Add sliced onions and fry until deep golden (15-18 mins). Add ginger-garlic paste and fry 3 mins.

steps.title: Add tomatoes & spices
steps.instruction: Add tomatoes, chilli powder, coriander powder, turmeric, salt. Cook until tomatoes break down and oil separates (8-10 mins).

steps.title: Add chicken
steps.instruction: Add chicken pieces. Mix well and sear on high heat 3-4 mins. Reduce heat, add ground masala paste, and mix well.

steps.title: Simmer
steps.instruction: Add ½ cup water. Cover and cook on low heat until chicken is tender (15-18 mins). Add garam masala and simmer 2 more mins.

tips: Kalpasi and marathi mokku are unique Chettinad spices — find at specialty stores
Slow cooking on low heat develops deep flavor
Serve with appam, idiyappam, or parota
recipes.nutrition_notes: Black pepper and other spices have anti-inflammatory and digestive properties. High protein from chicken.

recipes.name: Green Smoothie Bowl
recipes.description: Nutrient-dense green smoothie bowl topped with fruits and seeds — perfect for weight loss and skin health

steps.title: Blend base
steps.instruction: Blend frozen banana, spinach, mango, and coconut water until completely smooth. Add more coconut water if too thick.

steps.title: Pour
steps.instruction: Pour thick smoothie into a bowl.

steps.title: Toppings
steps.instruction: Arrange granola, fresh berries, banana slices, chia seeds, and flax seeds on top. Drizzle honey.

tips: Use frozen fruit for thick, creamy consistency
Eat with a spoon — the thickness is intentional
Swap granola with muesli for lower sugar option
recipes.nutrition_notes: Spinach and mango are rich in Vitamin C, A, and iron. Chia and flax seeds add omega-3 fatty acids.

meal_plan_templates.name: Diabetic-Friendly Day Plan
meal_plan_templates.description: Low GI, high fiber meal plan for managing blood sugar naturally

items: Jeera Water or Methi seeds water (soaked overnight)
early_morning.notes: Drink before brushing for best effect

lunch.notes: Large portion of dal, small rice portion

tips: Eat every 3-4 hours to maintain stable blood sugar
Never skip breakfast
Drink water before meals
Walk 15 mins after lunch and dinner

meal_plan_templates.name: High-Protein Muscle Building Plan
meal_plan_templates.description: Protein-rich meal plan designed for muscle growth and strength training

items: Peanut Butter (1 tbsp) with whole wheat bread

bedtime.notes: Casein protein for overnight muscle repair

tips: Consume protein within 30 mins of workout
Distribute protein evenly across all meals
Stay hydrated — 3-4L water daily
Track macros for first 2 weeks

meal_plan_templates.name: Weight Loss 1500 Calorie Plan
meal_plan_templates.description: Balanced calorie-deficit plan with satisfying, nutrient-dense Indian foods

breakfast.notes: High fiber keeps you full till lunch

dinner.notes: Light, early dinner is key for weight loss

tips: Eat dinner before 7:30 PM
Chew slowly and eat mindfully
Replace snacks with sprouts or fruits
Drink 2 glasses of water before every meal

meal_plan_templates.name: Heart-Healthy Plan
meal_plan_templates.description: Omega-3 rich, low saturated fat meal plan for cardiovascular health

breakfast.notes: Beta-glucan in oats lowers LDL cholesterol

lunch.notes: Salmon is the king of heart-healthy foods

dinner.notes: Mustard greens are excellent for heart health

tips: Limit salt to 2300mg (1 tsp) daily
Cook in olive or mustard oil only
Avoid all fried foods and processed snacks
Include fish 3x per week

meal_plan_templates.name: Student Budget Meal Plan (₹100/day)
meal_plan_templates.description: Nutritious, balanced meal plan designed for college students on a tight budget

items: Dal Rice (2 cups rice + 1 bowl dal)

tips: Buy a packet of peanuts/chana for cheap protein
Eggs (₹6 each) are the cheapest complete protein
Carry a water bottle — saves money and is healthy
Avoid instant noodles — no nutrition

meal_plan_templates.name: South Indian Traditional Day
meal_plan_templates.description: Classic South Indian foods across the day — balanced and authentic
tips: Use Sona Masuri or red rice for better nutrition than processed white rice
Sambar at every meal is your nutritional insurance
Homemade filter coffee is healthier than instant

meal_plan_templates.name: Anemia Recovery Plan
meal_plan_templates.description: Iron and folate-rich meal plan to combat iron-deficiency anemia

early_morning.notes: Vitamin C in amla dramatically boosts iron absorption

breakfast.notes: Methi and ragi are iron powerhouses

mid_morning.notes: Pomegranate seeds are very high in iron

lunch.notes: Iron + Vitamin C combination maximizes absorption

evening_snack.notes: Avoid tea/coffee within 1 hr of meals (blocks iron absorption)

dinner.notes: Eggs provide heme iron which is easily absorbed

tips: Avoid tea and coffee within 1 hour of meals
Vitamin C at every meal maximizes iron absorption
Cook in iron utensils — food absorbs trace iron
Get iron levels tested every 3 months

meal_plan_templates.name: Vegan Indian Plan
meal_plan_templates.description: Complete nutrition on a fully plant-based Indian diet with no deficiencies

items: Chia seeds (1 tbsp) in coconut water
early_morning.notes: Chia seeds are a complete protein source

lunch.notes: Complete protein from rice + legume combination

dinner.notes: Tofu + quinoa = complete amino acid profile

bedtime.notes: Calcium and B12 from fortified oat milk

tips: Take B12 supplement daily — only genuine deficiency risk on vegan diet
Soak nuts/seeds before eating for better absorption
Combine rice with lentils at most meals for complete protein
Get calcium from ragi, sesame, and fortified plant milk

meal_plan_templates.name: PCOS Management Plan
meal_plan_templates.description: Low GI, anti-inflammatory diet plan designed to manage PCOS symptoms

early_morning.notes: Spearmint tea may reduce testosterone levels

breakfast.notes: High protein, healthy fats to balance hormones

lunch.notes: Methi is proven to reduce insulin resistance

evening_snack.notes: Zinc in pumpkin seeds supports hormone balance

tips: Avoid refined carbs and sugar entirely
Eat every 3-4 hours to keep insulin stable
Exercise 30 mins/day is as effective as medication for PCOS
Cut out all processed foods, packaged juices

meal_plan_templates.name: Better Sleep Plan
meal_plan_templates.description: Foods rich in tryptophan, magnesium, and melatonin to improve sleep quality

early_morning.notes: Banana contains tryptophan precursor to serotonin

items: Oats Porridge with banana and dates

mid_morning.notes: Kiwi has been shown to improve sleep quality in studies

lunch.notes: Tryptophan from dal helps melatonin production

items: Tart cherry juice or coconut water

dinner.notes: Light, early dinner is critical for good sleep

bedtime.notes: Nutmeg has mild sedative properties — do not overdo

tips: Stop eating 2-3 hours before bed
Avoid caffeine after 2 PM
Keep dinner light and easily digestible
No screens 1 hour before sleep

meal_plan_templates.name: Post-Workout Recovery Plan
meal_plan_templates.description: Timed nutrition for athletes and gym-goers to maximize recovery and muscle synthesis

post_workout.notes: Within 30 mins of workout — critical window

breakfast.notes: Complete amino acids from eggs + probiotics

lunch.notes: Largest meal — highest carbs and protein

dinner.notes: Omega-3 from salmon reduces muscle inflammation

bedtime.notes: Casein protein — slow-digesting, feeds muscles all night

tips: Protein timing is as important as total protein
Don't skip post-workout nutrition — 30 min window
Hydrate with 500ml water during workout
Sleep 8 hours — growth hormone released during sleep

meal_plan_templates.name: Immunity Boost Plan
meal_plan_templates.description: Antioxidant and micronutrient-rich plan to strengthen immune function

items: Warm water with turmeric, ginger, and lemon

lunch.notes: Iron + antioxidants + Vitamin C for maximum immunity

dinner.notes: Vitamin K2, probiotics, zinc from greens

tips: Get 15 mins sunlight daily for Vitamin D
Probiotics in curd/kefir support gut immunity
Stress and poor sleep destroy immunity — manage both
Stay hydrated — mucous membranes are your first line of defense

meal_plan_templates.name: IBS & Digestive Health Plan
meal_plan_templates.description: Low-FODMAP inspired, gut-friendly meal plan for IBS and digestive issues

early_morning.notes: Jeera and bael are proven digestive aids

breakfast.notes: Banana and oats are gut-soothing, low FODMAP

lunch.notes: Moong dal is the most digestible legume

dinner.notes: Fermented idli is probiotic and gentle on stomach

tips: Avoid onion, garlic, cauliflower during flare-ups
Eat small meals every 2-3 hours
Chew food 30 times minimum
Identify and avoid personal trigger foods

meal_plan_templates.name: North Indian Vegetarian Plan
meal_plan_templates.description: Classic North Indian vegetarian diet with all regional favorites
tips: Replace white rice with jeera or brown rice
Add curd to every meal for probiotics
Use minimum ghee/butter to keep it balanced

meal_plan_templates.name: Kidney-Friendly Low-Phosphorus Plan
meal_plan_templates.description: Low potassium, low phosphorus, low sodium meal plan for kidney disease management

items: Boiled and drained vegetables in water (to leach potassium)
early_morning.notes: Boil and discard water for all vegetables to reduce potassium

mid_morning.notes: Apple is one of the safest fruits for kidney patients

lunch.notes: Lauki is among the safest vegetables for kidney disease

items: Cauliflower Stir Fry (boiled first, water discarded)
dinner.notes: Limit dal to small portion — high in phosphorus

tips: Always consult your nephrologist before changing diet
Boil and discard water from all vegetables to reduce potassium
Avoid bananas, tomatoes, potatoes, oranges (high potassium)
Avoid nuts, seeds, dairy, colas (high phosphorus)
